analysis: Of 20 critical/major open issues (1 critical, 19 major), only ISS-421 (WIN-012 new dome content) is WIN-specific. All 19 major issues are PROSE-LEVEL requiring document/section rewrites, not wins.json field patches. The decider identifies these issues but cannot generate patches against non-patchable content. Human editorial review is required.

CRITICAL_FACTUAL_ERROR.problem: Opening claim that d_geo is undefined is FALSE. Dome V13 page explicitly defines d_geo with formula. Requires complete rewrite of lead argument.
CRITICAL_FACTUAL_ERROR.fix_required: Editorial rewrite - reframe to: d_geo IS defined, but parameters are fitted post-hoc to WGS84 data, inputs are WGS84-derived, and n(r) remains undefined.

problems: Describes eclipse prediction as '-17 to -21 nT from 1.672x scaling' - but actual dome has dual-baseline (BOU: -5 to -13 nT; W004: -10 to -26 nT)
Claims 1.672 is eclipse scaling factor when it's actually KAPPA (mag-gravity coupling from WIN-012)
References GUA 2016 baseline when current baselines are BOU 2017 and W004 empirical
Claims code computes -29.1 nT but monitor.py not archived in raw-text/ - unverifiable
ECLIPSE_PREDICTION_MISCHARACTERIZATION.fix_required: Rewrite with accurate dual-baseline, per-station FSF description. Cite verifiable numbers only.

DOCX_STRUCTURAL_GAPS.problem: Sections 5.14, 5.15, 5.16 (Repository Infrastructure, Model Code Analysis, Monitoring) completely absent from generated DOCX. These are the review's most original sections.
DOCX_STRUCTURAL_GAPS.fix_required: Add all three sections to build-doc-v4.js prose generation. Currently hardcoded only in generate-html.js.

HARDCODED_STALE_COUNT.problem: Hardcodes Self-Contradicted count as 11 when wins.json now has 12. Violates project rule 'never hardcode numbers'.
HARDCODED_STALE_COUNT.fix_required: Replace hardcoded '11' with dynamic computation from wins.json.

problems: SEC-3.2/SEC-6.2 never address dome's E-PRED-B test (Hartland > Ebro despite lower coverage - dome's strongest eclipse counterargument)
SEC-4.8 never addresses dome's pre-built aetheric refraction defense in solar diameter argument
SEC-4.9 claims 6 WINs depend on aetheric refraction but WIN detail text never explicitly invokes refraction for WIN-017/026/033/056
MISSING_COUNTERARGUMENT_SECTIONS.fix_required: Expand sections to address counterarguments explicitly. Either add refraction language to WIN details, or soften SEC-4.9 claim.

problems: SEC-4.5.4 HTML says sun at H ≈ 8,537 km (firmament apex height) instead of sun altitude 5,733 km
SEC-4.5.3 claim about gravity contradiction needs correction
FACTUAL_ERRORS_IN_SECTIONS.fix_required: Correct numerical values in both HTML and DOCX prose sections.

problems: SEC-4.5.9 compares scaffold Euclidean distance (410 km) to rail distance (223 km) instead of great-circle (~160 km)
SEC-4.5.9 claims 'genuine model would have one version' (easily refuted by any iterative physics)
SEC-4.8 claims '50% larger at noon' AND '30% distance variation = 30% size variation' (mathematically inconsistent; actual is 48% reduction at 90° hour angle)
REDUNDANCY_AND_LOGICAL_ERRORS.fix_required: Section rewrites. Use correct comparison distances. Replace overstatement with actual geometric constraint argument.

problems: SEC-5.14 makes claims about Python scripts (inject_ai_layer.py, update_optical_caveats.py) with line references but source files not preserved in raw-text/
SEC-5.16 cites specific monitor.py code and formula, but monitor.py not archived - claims become unverifiable if dome author removes code
ARCHIVAL_GAPS_BLOCKING_VERIFICATION.fix_required: Archive all referenced Python source files from dome repository into raw-text/. Update raw-text-archive to include full source tree versions.

ISS-421.issue: Dome site now has section 'The Law of Aether Pressure' (appears new or newly indexed). May contain independent coupling constant derivation.
ISS-421.action: MONITORING — Verify new section content. If it introduces derivation differing from circular mass-density assumption, WIN-012 evidence may need expansion. Current tidal contradiction approach (WIN-045/046/049/050/051) remains valid.
ISS-421.reason: Requires content analysis and possible WIN detail text expansion, not automated patch.

next_steps: Human editorial review required for all 20 issues. Provide detailed fix descriptions from this report to human reviewer.
Issues ISS-415, ISS-082/84/128 are highest priority (credibility damage, factual errors in conclusion section).
Issues ISS-422, ISS-102 are structural and blocking DOCX generation - should be fixed before next build.
Monitor ISS-421 (WIN-012 new content) - may require data update once dome content is verified.
